vibe: High-end print magazine meets brutalist interactive web. Escape AI-slop completely. Inspired by Bloomberg Businessweek, FT Weekend, and Apartamento. Real depth with paper textures, ink-bleed aesthetics, and striking typography.

display.usage: Massive chapter numbers, brutalist headlines, and data-heavy structural elements.

serif.usage: Subheads, pull quotes, metaphors, marginalia, and long-form reading paragraphs. Crucial for the 'Financial Times' contrast.

mono.usage: Formulas, interactive calculators, numerical inputs, and small technical labels.

scales.h1_hero: text-6xl md:text-8xl font-black tracking-tighter leading-none uppercase
scales.h2_chapter: text-5xl md:text-7xl font-black tracking-tighter leading-none uppercase
scales.h3_editorial: text-3xl md:text-5xl font-serif font-bold italic tracking-tight leading-tight
scales.quote: text-2xl md:text-4xl font-serif italic leading-tight border-l-4 pl-6
scales.caption: text-xs md:text-sm font-mono tracking-widest uppercase

background.description: Warm newsprint / raw cream paper texture. NEVER use pure white.

text.description: Rich black ink (never #000000) for highest contrast.

ch1_simple.name: Electric Blue
ch1_simple.usage: Primary accent for 'Intérêts simples'. Use for graph lines, highlights, and drop caps.

layout_and_spacing.grid_system: Asymmetrical 12-column grid. Elements should explicitly span columns (e.g., col-span-4 vs col-span-8). Break symmetry constantly.
layout_and_spacing.spacing_scale: Extremely generous. Use p-12, p-16, p-24 for section padding to emulate print margins.
layout_and_spacing.borders: Brutalist. Use 1px or 2px solid #1C1C1A borders for structural division, exactly like a newspaper layout. No soft shadows. Only harsh dropshadows if any.

components.calculators: Should look like technical widgets. Solid borders, mono typography, stark input fields with no radius (rounded-none).
components.flashcards: Physical paper feel. 1px solid border, pale background, 3D flip on click using Framer Motion.
components.marginalia: Definitions and side notes should hang in the margins on desktop (absolute positioning or grid offset).
components.drop_caps: First letter of chapter paragraphs should be massive (float-left, text-6xl, font-serif, mt-2 mr-4).

motion.page_transitions: Hyperframes style: smooth vertical slide reveals overlapping the previous chapter, simulating a physical page turn or continuous print scroll.

scroll_animations.text_reveal: Words staggered reveal from bottom up using clip-path or overflow-hidden wrappers.
scroll_animations.graphs: SVG path drawing on view (strokeDasharray and pathLength from 0 to 1) to visually show mathematical growth curves.

motion.hover_states: Magnetic button hovers (small x/y displacement). Invert colors on hover (Black bg, cream text) for CTAs.

media_and_assets.style: Halftone, risograph, high contrast duotone ink over cream paper. Geometric, striking, and slightly abstract.

images.description: Cover art featuring abstract geometric charts overlapping in risograph textures.
Halftone fountain illustration for simple interest metaphor (Electric Blue duotone).
Halftone massive snowball illustration for compound interest metaphor (Neon Orange duotone).
Halftone pizza sliced by scalpel illustration for proportional rate metaphor (Bright Pink duotone).
Halftone balanced brass scale illustration for equivalent rate metaphor (Acid Green duotone).

instructions_to_main_agent: Build a single-page React application with an editorial table of contents acting as the 'Magazine Cover'.
Use 'Schibsted Grotesk' for chapter numbers (make them huge, overlapping content) and structural headers.
Use 'Cormorant Garamond' for the narrative text, pull quotes, and metaphors.
Use 'JetBrains Mono' for all formulas, numbers in the calculators, and variable definitions.
For formulas, you can use KaTeX or styled spans, but it MUST look typeset beautifully, not like raw code.
Each chapter MUST use its assigned spot color (from chapter_accents) for UI highlights, graph lines, and hover states to give visual distinction.
Calculators must be highly interactive (sliders). Output updates in real-time. Use stark, solid borders (rounded-none) for the inputs.
Graphs should be implemented with Framer Motion SVGs to show the line drawing across the chart (linear for simple interest, exponential for compound interest).
Include a 'Flashcard / Quiz' section at the end of each chapter with 3D flip animations.
DO NOT use generic Shadcn cards with soft shadows. Strip Shadcn components to their bare bones and apply the brutalist 1px solid border style.
